meta.title: TB-DF-6: Data Connections, Core Views, and Sharing

questions.stem: A candidate reviews a workbook requirement. A user needs to combine rows from monthly CSV files that share the same structure. Which Tableau data-source operation should be used?

options.explanation: A union is correct because it appends rows from tables with the same structure. Filters, story points, and dashboard actions do not combine source rows.

questions.stem: A team is designing a new solution. A workbook should contain the workbook plus local data files in one shareable package. Which file type should be saved?

options.explanation: A packaged workbook (`.twbx`) is correct because it bundles the workbook with local data resources. A `.twb` stores definitions only, and `.hyper` or `.csv` do not package the workbook.

questions.stem: During exam preparation, you evaluate this scenario. A field appears green and creates an axis. What does that usually indicate in Tableau?

options.text: It is a published permission rule
It is always a table calculation
It is being used as a continuous field
options.explanation: A continuous field is correct because green pills typically indicate continuous values that create axes. Hidden fields, permissions, and table calculations are separate concepts.

options.text: It is hidden from the workbook

questions.stem: An analyst needs the most appropriate next step. Which Marks card property controls the hue of marks in a view?

options.explanation: Color is correct because the Color property controls mark hue and color encoding. Detail changes granularity, Tooltip affects hover text, and Rows affects layout.

questions.stem: A practitioner wants to avoid a common mistake. A top-N filter should be evaluated only after a primary category filter is applied. What should the analyst add?

options.explanation: A context filter is correct because it creates a primary subset that later filters and top-N logic can depend on. Packaging, unions, and map layers do not affect filter order in this way.

questions.stem: A candidate reviews a workbook requirement. An analyst wants a reusable ratio built from aggregated values in the view. Which Tableau feature is generally appropriate?

options.explanation: A calculated field is correct because Tableau uses calculated fields to derive reusable metrics and dimensions. Story points, permissions, and highlight actions do not create reusable formulas.

questions.stem: A team is designing a new solution. A workbook must query a changing operational source without creating an extract first. Which connection mode best fits?

options.explanation: A live connection is correct because it queries the source system directly rather than using an extract copy. Story sheets, set actions, and packaged workbooks are unrelated to connection mode.

questions.stem: During exam preparation, you evaluate this scenario. A workbook author wants others to interact with the visualizations in a browser after publication. Where is the content typically published?

options.explanation: Tableau Server or Tableau Cloud is correct because published workbooks can be consumed interactively in a browser there. Screenshots, hidden tabs, and the Data Source page do not publish interactive content.

questions.stem: An analyst needs the most appropriate next step. A user wants a chart that shows magnitude by bar length across categories. Which basic chart type is usually the clearest choice?

options.explanation: A bar chart is correct because it is a clear default for comparing values across categories. Packed bubbles, Gantt charts, and containers are not the best general starting point for simple comparisons.

options.text: A Gantt chart for every scenario

questions.stem: A practitioner wants to avoid a common mistake. How are dimensions and measures typically different in Tableau?

options.text: Dimensions are always numeric and measures are always text
Dimensions cannot appear on the Marks card
Dimensions usually segment or label data, while measures are typically aggregated quantitative values
options.explanation: Dimensions-versus-measures is correct because dimensions often partition data and measures are quantitative fields that can be aggregated. The other statements are oversimplified or false.

questions.stem: A candidate reviews a workbook requirement. A dashboard should let one chart filter another when a user clicks a mark. Which feature should be configured?

options.explanation: A filter action is correct because dashboard actions can pass selections from one view to another as filters. Unions, refresh schedules, and role conversion do not create interactive cross-filtering.

questions.stem: A team is designing a new solution. A date field should support drill-down from year to quarter to month. Which Tableau structure is most relevant?

options.explanation: A hierarchy is correct because it organizes fields into drillable levels such as Year to Quarter to Month. Story captions, tooltips, and unions do not provide drill paths.

questions.stem: During exam preparation, you evaluate this scenario. A user saves a `.twb` file instead of `.twbx`. What is the key limitation?

options.text: It does not bundle the local data files and related resources
options.explanation: A `.twb` limitation is correct because it stores workbook definitions but does not package local data files. It can still contain worksheets and calculated fields.

options.text: It forces all fields to become dimensions

questions.stem: An analyst needs the most appropriate next step. A source contains related tables that should remain separate logical tables instead of being flattened prematurely. Which modeling concept is most relevant in modern Tableau?

options.explanation: Relationships are correct because they connect logical tables while preserving separate table context until query time. Cross joins, story points, and duplication do not provide that semantic modeling approach.

questions.stem: A practitioner wants to avoid a common mistake. A map appears automatically after dragging a recognized geographic field. Why does Tableau do this?

options.text: Maps require a packaged workbook file type
Tableau can interpret fields with geographic roles and plot them spatially
options.explanation: Geographic-role interpretation is correct because Tableau recognizes geographic fields and can generate maps from them. The other options confuse geography with unrelated features.

options.text: Every dimension is automatically a time series

questions.stem: A team is designing a new solution. Several files have identical columns and must be stacked into one table. What should the analyst create?

options.explanation: A union is correct because it appends rows from tables with the same structure. Filters, story points, and dashboard actions do not combine source rows.

questions.stem: During exam preparation, you evaluate this scenario. A user wants to email a Tableau file that includes local resources and extracts together. Which format is best?

options.explanation: A packaged workbook (`.twbx`) is correct because it bundles the workbook with local data resources. A `.twb` stores definitions only, and `.hyper` or `.csv` do not package the workbook.

questions.stem: An analyst needs the most appropriate next step. What does a green pill most commonly represent in a Tableau view?

options.text: It is a published permission rule
It is always a table calculation
It is being used as a continuous field
options.explanation: A continuous field is correct because green pills typically indicate continuous values that create axes. Hidden fields, permissions, and table calculations are separate concepts.

options.text: It is hidden from the workbook

questions.stem: A practitioner wants to avoid a common mistake. A chart needs different colors for categories. Which Marks card property should the analyst use?

options.explanation: Color is correct because the Color property controls mark hue and color encoding. Detail changes granularity, Tooltip affects hover text, and Rows affects layout.

questions.stem: A candidate reviews a workbook requirement. Which filter type establishes a temporary subset that later filters can depend on?

options.explanation: A context filter is correct because it creates a primary subset that later filters and top-N logic can depend on. Packaging, unions, and map layers do not affect filter order in this way.

questions.stem: A team is designing a new solution. A worksheet needs a custom formula such as profit ratio. What should the user create?

options.explanation: A calculated field is correct because Tableau uses calculated fields to derive reusable metrics and dimensions. Story points, permissions, and highlight actions do not create reusable formulas.

questions.stem: During exam preparation, you evaluate this scenario. Data must stay queried from the source system instead of being copied into an extract. What should the user choose?

options.explanation: A live connection is correct because it queries the source system directly rather than using an extract copy. Story sheets, set actions, and packaged workbooks are unrelated to connection mode.

questions.stem: An analyst needs the most appropriate next step. To share interactive content broadly, what should the author use?

options.explanation: Tableau Server or Tableau Cloud is correct because published workbooks can be consumed interactively in a browser there. Screenshots, hidden tabs, and the Data Source page do not publish interactive content.

questions.stem: A practitioner wants to avoid a common mistake. To compare values across discrete categories, which view is often the best first option?

options.explanation: A bar chart is correct because it is a clear default for comparing values across categories. Packed bubbles, Gantt charts, and containers are not the best general starting point for simple comparisons.

options.text: A Gantt chart for every scenario

questions.stem: A candidate reviews a workbook requirement. What best describes the role distinction between dimensions and measures?

options.text: Dimensions are always numeric and measures are always text
Dimensions cannot appear on the Marks card
Dimensions usually segment or label data, while measures are typically aggregated quantitative values
options.explanation: Dimensions-versus-measures is correct because dimensions often partition data and measures are quantitative fields that can be aggregated. The other statements are oversimplified or false.

questions.stem: A team is designing a new solution. What allows user interaction in one worksheet to filter related views on a dashboard?

options.explanation: A filter action is correct because dashboard actions can pass selections from one view to another as filters. Unions, refresh schedules, and role conversion do not create interactive cross-filtering.

questions.stem: During exam preparation, you evaluate this scenario. Users want to expand dates through ordered levels in the view. What should the analyst rely on?

options.explanation: A hierarchy is correct because it organizes fields into drillable levels such as Year to Quarter to Month. Story captions, tooltips, and unions do not provide drill paths.

questions.stem: An analyst needs the most appropriate next step. Why might a plain workbook file be harder to share with someone who lacks the original local data files?

options.text: It does not bundle the local data files and related resources
options.explanation: A `.twb` limitation is correct because it stores workbook definitions but does not package local data files. It can still contain worksheets and calculated fields.

options.text: It forces all fields to become dimensions

questions.stem: A practitioner wants to avoid a common mistake. An analyst wants to preserve table-level context between related tables. What should be used on the Data Source page?

options.explanation: Relationships are correct because they connect logical tables while preserving separate table context until query time. Cross joins, story points, and duplication do not provide that semantic modeling approach.

questions.stem: A candidate reviews a workbook requirement. What enables Tableau to create a map when a field is assigned a geographic role?

options.text: Maps require a packaged workbook file type
Tableau can interpret fields with geographic roles and plot them spatially
options.explanation: Geographic-role interpretation is correct because Tableau recognizes geographic fields and can generate maps from them. The other options confuse geography with unrelated features.

options.text: Every dimension is automatically a time series

questions.stem: During exam preparation, you evaluate this scenario. A workbook must append like-for-like tables from multiple files. Which operation is correct?

options.explanation: A union is correct because it appends rows from tables with the same structure. Filters, story points, and dashboard actions do not combine source rows.

questions.stem: An analyst needs the most appropriate next step. What file type bundles the workbook definition with local data assets for sharing?

options.explanation: A packaged workbook (`.twbx`) is correct because it bundles the workbook with local data resources. A `.twb` stores definitions only, and `.hyper` or `.csv` do not package the workbook.

options.text: It is a published permission rule
It is always a table calculation
It is being used as a continuous field
options.explanation: A continuous field is correct because green pills typically indicate continuous values that create axes. Hidden fields, permissions, and table calculations are separate concepts.

options.text: It is hidden from the workbook

options.explanation: Color is correct because the Color property controls mark hue and color encoding. Detail changes granularity, Tooltip affects hover text, and Rows affects layout.

questions.stem: A team is designing a new solution. A worksheet needs other filters to operate within a chosen subset of data. Which feature is most relevant?

options.explanation: A context filter is correct because it creates a primary subset that later filters and top-N logic can depend on. Packaging, unions, and map layers do not affect filter order in this way.

questions.stem: During exam preparation, you evaluate this scenario. To derive a new metric from existing fields, what should the analyst use?

options.explanation: A calculated field is correct because Tableau uses calculated fields to derive reusable metrics and dimensions. Story points, permissions, and highlight actions do not create reusable formulas.

questions.stem: An analyst needs the most appropriate next step. When the goal is to query the source directly each time, which connection type is appropriate?

options.explanation: A live connection is correct because it queries the source system directly rather than using an extract copy. Story sheets, set actions, and packaged workbooks are unrelated to connection mode.

questions.stem: A practitioner wants to avoid a common mistake. Which platform is commonly used to publish Tableau workbooks for browser-based consumption?

options.explanation: Tableau Server or Tableau Cloud is correct because published workbooks can be consumed interactively in a browser there. Screenshots, hidden tabs, and the Data Source page do not publish interactive content.

questions.stem: A candidate reviews a workbook requirement. A simple categorical comparison is needed for exam-style analysis. Which chart is typically most appropriate?

options.explanation: A bar chart is correct because it is a clear default for comparing values across categories. Packed bubbles, Gantt charts, and containers are not the best general starting point for simple comparisons.

options.explanation: A hierarchy is correct because it organizes fields into drillable levels such as Year to Quarter to Month. Story captions, tooltips, and unions do not provide drill paths.
